rulings.Case Title: LYON FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC. DBA U.S. BANCORP VS. POPKOFF FROZEN FOODS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursant To Stipulation
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 28, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS LYON FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC. DBA U.S. BANCORP, AND U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION'S Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursant To Stipulation. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH M. FEE et al VS. ANDREW B. GIANG MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 28, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFFS JOSEPH FEE, LAURA REES, AND NANCY FEE'S Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint. This motion will be heard by Judge Kahn at 9:15 am in Dept. 302. Judge Kahn has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. Plaintiffs have adequately pled and submitted sufficient admissible evidence to support their requests for punitive damages against all defendants for the causes of action covered by the motion. Plaintiffs' evidence shows that there are triable facts whether: 1) the individual defendants acted in conscious disregard of the rights of Elizabeth Fee and 2) whether the entity defendants either had advance knowledge of the unfitness of the individual defendants or authorized or ratified the misconduct of the individual defendants or committed their own misconduct. Because the issues on this motion do not involve the duty or standard of care of a health care professional, plaintiffs were not required to provide expert evidence to support their motion. Their percipient evidence is sufficient to support the minimal evidentiary burden on this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: LAUREN HAULE VS. NEW DEAL DESIGN LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Reopening Discovery And Compelling Attendance At Deposition And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 28, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT NEW DEAL DESIGN LLC'S Motion For An Order Reopening Discovery And Compelling Attendance At Deposition And For Sanctions. Denied. CCP sec. 2025.610 provides that a natural person, including a party to the action, can only be deposed once. If a party wants to take a subsequent deposition of a natural person, it can do so only after obtaining leave of court for good cause shown or if the parties stipulate that a subsequent deposition may be taken. (CCP sec. 20225.610(b).) Defendant already deposed Plaintiff for three days. Defendant failed to comply with CCP sec. 2025.610 and therefore, the motion to compel an improperly noticed deposition is denied. The notice was also defective because Plaintiff resided out of state at the time the notice was served. (CCP secs. 1989 and 2025.250(a).) The motion for an order reopening discovery is denied. The evidence establishes that Defendant did not act diligently in pursuing additionally discovery. Pursuant to CCP sec. 2024.050(c), Defendant shall pay Plaintiff sanctions in the amount of $4,085 within 30 days of entry of this order. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
